attack_memo: A hostile reviewer would argue that TRACE-BiOpt risks over-selling a broad traffic sensor placement method while proving and testing a narrower transparent GLS/MAP reconstruction protocol. The current text answers the attack by scoping the method, theorem certificates, baseline registry, and evidence claims to that protocol.

checks.evidence: PROOF_AUDIT, PAPER_CLAIM_AUDIT, and CITATION_AUDIT must all be PASS.
Method section and abstract/highlights distinguish TRACE-BiOpt from a baseline pool selector.
Appendix discloses the fixed registry and strongest-comparator protocol.
Any row with mixed paired evidence must be caveated in abstract/results/table/appendix wording.
Exchange theorem and appendix scope the certificate to searched active-set moves.
Appendix explicitly addresses the strongest scope-mismatch rejection path.
Core paper text has 7205 words across main theory/results/discussion/appendix.

net_assessment: The worst scope/evidence attack is answered by current text for submission-gate purposes; remaining risks are author metadata and optional external human review.
